title: Parse a sort query parameter
prompt_md:
Many APIs accept a sort param like `?sort=-created` (leading `-` means descending). Write `normalize_sort(param)` that turns the raw string into a dict:

- `"-created"` -> `{"field": "created", "dir": "desc"}`
- `"name"`     -> `{"field": "name", "dir": "asc"}`

A leading `"-"` means descending and must be stripped from the field name. No leading `"-"` means ascending.
hints: If it starts with `-`, slice it off with `param[1:]` to get the field.
